description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, commonly found in urban environments for emergency water access. The hydrant features a domed top with a central operating nut, a cylindrical body, and a hexagonal base for stability. Three hose connection outlets are visible: two on the sides and one facing forward, each with a prominent cap. The surface is painted a bright, glossy red, with subtle paneling and ridges for structural reinforcement. The hydrant appears to be constructed primarily from cast iron or steel, designed to withstand high pressure and outdoor conditions. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the object is shown isolated without any surrounding curb or sidewalk.
description: A bright red, metal fire hydrant with a domed top, three hose outlets, and a hexagonal base, designed for emergency water access.
description_view_0: The hydrant is seen from an angle showing the front outlet and one side outlet, with the operating nut on top clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view shows the hydrant head-on, displaying all three hose outlets and the symmetrical design of the caps.
description_view_2: The hydrant is viewed from the opposite side, highlighting the side outlet and the smooth cylindrical body.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the back of the hydrant, with the two side outlets visible and the main body uninterrupted by outlets.
